edge_hypothesis: Supertrend direction-flip is the highest-quality entry signal in the system (7 ACCEPTs, PF=1.921 champion at 8:1 R:R). 12:1 R:R was validated on RSI slingshot (PF=1.270, 162 trades) but has NEVER been tested on Supertrend. Supertrend's signal-to-noise ratio should be BETTER than RSI's for extreme R:R because: (a) Supertrend only fires on trend reversal, not pullbacks, producing fewer but higher-conviction entries, (b) the ADX>20 gate already filters choppy markets where stops get hit before trends develop. At 12:1, expected win rate drops to ~7-9% but each winner = 12x risk. If crypto trends on ETH regularly extend to 24x ATR from reversal point (which 4h data should confirm given the 85-trade sample at 8:1 showed 18% win rate), this becomes the single best risk-adjusted strategy. The ADX=15 variant tests whether relaxing the trend strength filter adds enough extra winners to compensate for slightly noisier entries. Invalidation: if PF drops below 1.2 at 12:1, the 8:1 sweet spot was optimal and trends don't extend beyond 8x ATR frequently enough.

variants.name: supertrend_extreme_tail_12to1_4h
variants.description: Supertrend direction flip + ADX>20 on ETH/4h with 12:1 R:R. The champion template (PF=1.921 at 8:1) pushed to extreme tail harvesting. ADX 20 is the proven gate level from 7 ACCEPT outcomes. 2.0x ATR stop meets STOP_FLOOR. 24.0x ATR TP = 12:1 R:R. Expected win rate ~7-9%, but the Supertrend signal quality should sustain this because trend reversals captured by Supertrend direction flip are structural, not noise.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (24.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
Hypothesis failure mode: if PF < 1.2, Supertrend signals don't extend to 12x ATR frequently enough — revert to 8:1
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 24.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: SUPERTd_7_3.0 direction flip — structural trend reversal signal
ADX_14 > 20 — proven gate level from 7 ACCEPT outcomes
ATR_14 for 2.0x stop / 24.0x TP — 12:1 R:R extreme tail harvesting

variants.name: supertrend_extreme_tail_12to1_1h
variants.description: Supertrend 12:1 on ETH/1h. The 1h timeframe captures more Supertrend flips than 4h, producing more trades. Supertrend at 8:1 on 1h achieved PF=1.339 (ACCEPT). Testing whether the signal quality holds at 12:1 on the faster timeframe. More trades = more statistical confidence in the 12:1 R:R thesis.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (24.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
Hypothesis failure mode: if 1h win rate < 5%, the shorter timeframe produces too many false Supertrend flips for 12:1
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 24.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: SUPERTd_7_3.0 direction flip on 1h — more trades than 4h

variants.name: supertrend_relaxed_gate_12to1
variants.description: Supertrend 12:1 with relaxed ADX gate (15 instead of 20) on ETH/4h. The ultra variant (ADX=10) achieved PF=1.907 at 8:1 by capturing more entries in lower-trend-strength environments. ADX=15 is the midpoint between standard (20) and ultra (10). At 12:1 R:R, the extra entries from relaxed ADX may add profitable trades in transitional regimes where trends start slow then accelerate past 12x ATR. Tests: does looser entry + extreme R:R = good or bad?
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (24.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
Hypothesis failure mode: if PF < ultra_8to1 (1.907), the relaxed gate adds noise that 12:1 can't overcome
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 24.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: ADX_14 > 15 — relaxed gate, midpoint between standard (20) and ultra (10)
